[2018-07-09 20:36] amalloy: @WillFlame generally hannibull/shredder are best when you still get full absorb after your opponent targets them down
[2018-07-09 20:36] amalloy: like if your opponent can usefully attack for exactly 4, shredder will be a liability. but if they're attacking for 6 and your absorber is wall, then having one shredder will be great
[2018-07-09 20:37] amalloy: hannibull is so gigantic you can sometimes get away with buying it before your opponent can kill it at all, and you just accept that you'll lose absorb value on the turn they do kill it; in the mean time you got good value. for shredder that's rarely very good
[2018-07-09 20:39] cogito3 (Cogito): usually you want to block with shredder or hannibull, but you can attack if you're killing something good with the attack
[2018-07-09 20:39] amalloy: or if you unexpectedly wound up overdefended by 4, you might as well attack
[2018-07-09 20:41] willflame: in the case where I'm attacking, do I only kill Shredder/Hannibull when they deny absorb?
[2018-07-09 20:41] willflame: Same with Iceblade Golem actually, I never know whether to pressure their defences or help out my own
[2018-07-09 20:42] amalloy: iceblade is different because it's not a blocker
[2018-07-09 20:43] amalloy: if the enemy shredder/bull are blocking and you can kill them with the damage you have, then you should always do so. this is because if you don't, your opponent can choose whether to lose it or not, and they will choose whichever is stronger for them. by killing it, you remove the choice
[2018-07-09 20:43] amalloy: it's less clear what to do about iceblades, or bull/shredder that aren't blocking this turn

[2018-07-10 00:02] zheliu (Zhe): Imo shredder is pretty good... think of it as buying your walls for next turn prematurely but each one has 4 hp instead of 3 and it pressures 1 extra damage if you end up not needing the defense, but the downside is that if you buy the shredders early anticipating damage and the opponent actually does attack then you'll be one turn behind on buying damage units instead of if you had bought damage units the previous turn then walls the following turn 

Also like the other guy said frontline units like shredder and polywall are most efficient when the opponent isn't attacking for exact... that way you can soak the most damage possible

For example if opponent has 12 attack it's better to build one polywall and soak some damage on another unit instead but if enemy has 11 damage it may be good to buy two polywalls to soak 5

[2018-07-10 17:30] medar: Took a quick look, and I think the biggest mistake was all of those early front line units. The only thing they did was allow your opponent to deny absorb.
[2018-07-10 17:30] cachotognax: makes sense, they aslo got absorbed
[2018-07-10 17:31] cachotognax: yeah it was dumb indeed
[2018-07-10 17:31] cachotognax: also I underestimated the synthetizer
[2018-07-10 17:31] medar: You are right that it's definitely a very slow set, so no hurry to build attack. Getting all drones should be correct.
[2018-07-10 17:33] medar: Hannibulls are great soak, but so are Bombarders, Aegis and Shredder which are easier to use. Might be some spots to use Hannibulls too  ofc.
[2018-07-10 17:33] cachotognax: also bombarders or steelsplitters? I got them to get abouve soak, but they lack of permanence was heavy, I guess I should have gotten gauss cannons?

[2018-07-10 17:35] medar: not sure how it works out for splitters and cannons with synth

[2018-07-10 19:27] amalloy: how do i link to a previous message? there was a discussion about frontline basics in this channel 23 hours ago

[2018-07-10 19:35] willflame: is it worth holding Scorchilla's against Plexo Cell's?
[2018-07-10 19:35] willflame: if your damage otherwise is < 4
[2018-07-10 19:36] cachotognax: if you hold less than 4 scorches and you have no other damage source yes.
[2018-07-10 19:37] mrguy888: on average you wanna hold 1 scorch per absorb you are denying
[2018-07-10 19:37] cachotognax: I see it as holding a scorch=wasting 1 damage.
[2018-07-10 19:37] amalloy: as a rule of thumb, it is reasonable to hold 1 scorch to deny 1 absorb, or 2 scorches to deny 2 absorb
[2018-07-10 19:37] amalloy: i guess mrguy beat me to it
[2018-07-10 19:37] cachotognax: oh I was right all along, cool!
[2018-07-10 19:40] willflame: is it the same for holding Immolite?
[2018-07-10 19:40] cachotognax: holding immo is wasting half damage.
[2018-07-10 19:42] cachotognax: if all you are doing with that immolite is holding it for several turns, maybe you could have done something else with your resources.
[2018-07-10 19:42] willflame: no, i'd just be holding it for 1 turn in order to sync with Scorchillas
[2018-07-10 19:45] simmarine (sim): @amalloy ill go dive for it, thanks for the heads up
[2018-07-10 19:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Buying Immolite to sync with Scorchilla doesn't seem very right to me...
[2018-07-10 19:47] simmarine (sim): i once bought an iso kronus to do a timing with some scorchillas, im not really sure if it was optimal though lol
[2018-07-10 19:50] supergreyman (Greyman): It would take 6 turns for everyone to be synced again. :Time:

[2018-07-10 20:01] simmarine (sim): i think one thing so far has been looking at a unit and not knowing why its good. and then the bot makes it and im suddenly wondering how the heck im supposed to even begin to deal with said unit
[2018-07-10 20:03] amalloy: often, especially as a new player, it's not really a question of reacting to a specific unit, but just fixing up your fundamentals. new players tend to be building the wrong number of drones, the wrong amount of tech, stuff like that. and if you're doing that, even bad units will feel unbeatable
[2018-07-10 20:05] simmarine (sim): i know finding the correct drone count will be hard for a good while
[2018-07-10 20:06] simmarine (sim): its satisfying being a half dozen drones down and eventually winning, but i always wonder if a really good player wouldve been able to navigate out of a situation like that
[2018-07-10 20:06] amalloy: well, half a dozen drones down is often the right number!
[2018-07-10 20:06] amalloy: you often win master bot games because he builds 10 more drones than he should
[2018-07-10 20:09] amalloy: actually rrFFK-1Xoxh is a cool example of that against a human. i stopped at 11 drones, my opponent went to 16, and we slogged it out there for ages

3CE
[2018-07-10 21:03] cogito3 (Cogito): and I think we're in a pretty good spot

[2018-07-10 21:11] amalloy: @Cogito DD DA TTD T1 TTD 11

[2018-07-10 21:12] amalloy: DD DA TTD TR T1 TTD 11

[2018-07-11 01:56] chiruver (AJ): is there a general way to deal with aggro when you a higher econ? Just spam buying walls doesn't seem to be enough to stop it if your opponent buys 2 tarsiers a turn
[2018-07-11 01:57] chiruver (AJ): I'm a really new player btw, i can defeat adept bot most of the time now, and expert bot occasionally, but still wet around the ears.
[2018-07-11 01:59] terroking: It really depends on the set, but you're correct in that tarsiers beat out walls. If there are better defenses in the extra set though, like centurion, doomed wall, aegis, ect. then that can give high econ the advantage
[2018-07-11 02:00] swapgo: Yep, basically you go high econ when there's good absorb, because it makes the early tarsiers worse
[2018-07-11 02:00] terroking: Keep in mind extra aggressive units can also exist, like electrovore, smorcus, grimbotcg
[2018-07-11 02:00] swapgo: Shock units are also a way to deter rushes, basically anything that hits for a ton quickly, but not necessarily consistently
[2018-07-11 02:00] swapgo: things like Plasmafier, Drake, Grenade Mech, Tesla Coil, etc.
[2018-07-11 02:00] swapgo: It might force them to slow down
[2018-07-11 02:00] f300xen (f0x): sword econ > drone econ, but people have to pay a certain amount to get that sword econ working
[2018-07-11 02:00] terroking: ^red has bad defense in general
[2018-07-11 02:01] f300xen (f0x): that certain amount is the absorb barrier

[2018-07-11 19:34] jonshea: Backing up to the discussion about holding Immolites to sync them with Scorches, new players do that kind of thing not because they want to deny absorb (a concept they don’t understand or know how to value), but because they think “more damage is harder to defend than less damage, and I win when they can’t defend anymore”. New players do not realize that when you hold back attack, that means your opponent doesn’t have to rebuild defense, which means your opponent will instead add more attack, which is usually a bad trade.
[2018-07-11 19:34] jonshea: At least, that’s how I thought when I was somewhat newer, and I assume I am a fairly typical new player.

messages.author: jonshea
messages.content: Backing up to the discussion about holding Immolites to sync them with Scorches, new players do that kind of thing not because they want to deny absorb (a concept they don’t understand or know how to value), but because they think “more damage is harder to defend than less damage, and I win when they can’t defend anymore”. New players do not realize that when you hold back attack, that means your opponent doesn’t have to rebuild defense, which means your opponent will instead add more attack, which is usually a bad trade.

[2018-07-11 19:56] willflame: that's not always the case, in particular, when you sync a lot of attack and the opponent buys non-blocking attackers with the extra gold

If you have 5 isos a weaker opponent might see they aren't under attack, they don't need to spend resources on defense. [2018-07-12 21:13] torikunsnaps (Torikun): so it's good to absorb on 1 stamina bombarder but I can't really articulate why?
[2018-07-12 21:13] torikunsnaps (Torikun): does stamina make bombarder that much different from attacking with redeemer?
[2018-07-12 21:14] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah. When it has 1 stamina they have to guard against the threat. If you attack twice it loses the threat, so opponent no longer needs extra defense
[2018-07-12 21:14] cogito3 (Cogito): you still might want to attack with it if it kills something good though
[2018-07-12 21:16] torikunsnaps (Torikun): so basically if bombarder had infinite threat it's better to always click on it but limited clicks tilt math towards defense tempo being more worth it?
[2018-07-12 21:17] cogito3 (Cogito): it's not really about the math or defense tempo. Think of it like this: you have 10 attack (w/ bombarder), so your opponent has to make 11 defense, right?
[2018-07-12 21:18] cogito3 (Cogito): now let's say you attack with the bombarder (say it's wall absorb). They're left with 3 defense. But now you only have 7 attack, since bombarder is out of stamina. So they only have to make 4 defense
[2018-07-12 21:18] cogito3 (Cogito): now let's say you don't attack with bombarder. They're left with 6 defense, but you still have 10 threat, so they still have to make 4 defense
[2018-07-12 21:18] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think stamina makes clicking bomb slightly worse but it's still so good to click generally
[2018-07-12 21:18] cogito3 (Cogito): well you want to click it *eventually*, but only so that you can then soak with it
[2018-07-12 21:18] Deleted User (Deleted User): exception being 1 stam ofc
[2018-07-12 21:19] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah this is all assuming 1 stam, if it has 2 you almost always want to click it
[2018-07-12 21:19] cogito3 (Cogito): anyway the point is, when it has 1 stam, clicking bombarder doesn't actually give you any more threat than holding it, so you might as well hold it
[2018-07-12 21:19] cogito3 (Cogito): the exceptions are: (a) it's killing something good like a rhino, or (b) you want to soak with it next turn
[2018-07-12 21:23] cogito3 (Cogito): (I should specify: by "something good" I mean a unit that does something for the opponent aside from defend, like rhino, drone, etc.)
[2018-07-12 21:24] cogito3 (Cogito): and of course if it's breaching you almost always want to click the 1 stam bombarder
[2018-07-12 21:25] torikunsnaps (Torikun): yeah basically stamina makes the threat worth more than damage, never thought of it that way
[2018-07-12 21:26] cogito3 (Cogito): it's not worth "more," it's worth the same, that's why you might as well just absorb for 3
[2018-07-12 21:27] cachotognax: it's basically worth the same: most attackers are a real problem since you have to rebuild defenses each turn, if you attack with 1 stamina, next turn the enemy doens't have to rebuild that defense, so it does nothing if the enemy is soaking on walls only, I'm excluding absorb ad exploit from the equation, they could paly a factor.
